title: World Geography
description: Question batch for the Physical Geography domain.

questions.question: What is the highest mountain range on Earth?
questions.explanation: The Himalayas contain the world's highest peaks.

questions.question: Which ocean is the largest?
questions.explanation: The Pacific is the largest ocean.

questions.question: Which is the longest river by common school reference?
questions.explanation: The Nile is widely taught as the longest river.

questions.question: Which desert is the largest hot desert?
questions.explanation: The Sahara is the largest hot desert.

questions.question: Which layer of Earth is beneath the crust?
questions.explanation: The mantle lies below the crust.

questions.question: What is a valley?
answers: A low area between higher land
questions.explanation: A valley is a low area between hills or mountains.

questions.question: What is an isthmus?
answers: A narrow strip of land connecting larger areas
questions.explanation: An isthmus links two larger land areas.

questions.question: What is a peninsula?
answers: Land surrounded by water on three sides
questions.explanation: A peninsula extends into water on three sides.

questions.question: Which process shapes land by wearing it away?
questions.explanation: Erosion removes and moves rock and soil.

questions.question: What causes tides mainly?
questions.explanation: Tides are driven mainly by the Moon's gravity.

questions.question: Which tectonic boundary often creates mountains?
questions.explanation: Colliding plates often build mountains.

questions.question: What is magma called after reaching the surface?
questions.explanation: Magma is called lava at the surface.

questions.question: Which biome is dominated by trees and heavy rainfall near the equator?
questions.explanation: Tropical rainforests are warm and wet year-round.

questions.question: Which biome is marked by permafrost?
questions.explanation: Tundra often includes permanently frozen ground.

questions.question: What is a watershed?
answers: An area drained by one river system
questions.explanation: A watershed drains water to a common outlet.

questions.question: Which current warms parts of Western Europe?
questions.explanation: The Gulf Stream helps moderate Western Europe's climate.

questions.question: What is the ring of active volcanoes around the Pacific called?
questions.explanation: The Pacific Ring of Fire has frequent volcanoes and quakes.

questions.question: Which force drives winds from high to low pressure?
questions.explanation: Air moves from high to low pressure.

questions.question: What is a delta?
answers: Land built by sediment at a river mouth
questions.explanation: A delta forms where sediment builds up near a river mouth.

questions.question: Which process changes water vapor into liquid droplets?
questions.explanation: Condensation forms liquid water from vapor.

questions.question: Why are coastal climates often milder?
answers: Water heats and cools more slowly than land
questions.explanation: Large bodies of water moderate temperature.

questions.question: What creates a rain shadow?
questions.explanation: Mountains force air up, leaving dry air on the far side.

questions.question: Why are river valleys often fertile?
questions.explanation: Rivers deposit nutrient-rich sediment.

questions.question: What causes earthquakes most directly?
questions.explanation: Earthquakes happen when stress is released along faults.

questions.question: Why is the equator generally warmer?
answers: It is closer to the Moon
questions.explanation: Sunlight strikes the equator more directly year-round.

questions.question: Which landform is raised flat land higher than nearby areas?
questions.explanation: A plateau is elevated and relatively flat.

questions.question: What role do glaciers play in landscapes?
questions.explanation: Glaciers erode rock and shape valleys.

questions.question: Why are many volcanoes near plate boundaries?
answers: Plate motion creates paths for magma
questions.explanation: Plate interactions help magma reach the surface.

questions.question: What is weathering?
questions.explanation: Weathering breaks rock down in place.

questions.question: Which feature marks where a river begins?
questions.explanation: A river starts at its source.
